channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Latest $3.8 billion RWA recovery shows how quickly DeFi absorbed the KelpDAO shock
item.description: Active use of tokenized real-world assets in DeFi has returned to about $3.77 billion, close to the level visible before an April 18 exploit triggered a $13 billion decline across DeFi as a whole in 4...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.description: Members will direct funding independently, with the consortium taking no role in Bitcoin governance or protocol decisions....

item.title: EU Hits Russia With Toughest Crypto Crackdown Yet
item.description: The EU can now ban crypto trades to entire nations for being in breach of Russian sanctions, and 11 crypto platforms have been targeted by the new measures....

item.title: EU fines Google $1 billion for search, app store antitrust violations
item.description: The European Commission fined Google €890 million ($1 billion) on Thursday after finding the company had violated the European Union's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which ensures fair online competition....

item.title: How Synthetic Identity Fraud is Coming for Machine Identities
item.description: Most people understand identity theft as an attacker stealing a real person's sensitive information and impersonating them. Synthetic identity fraud is much harder to catch. Instead of stealing a real...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
item.description: A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Crypto catches its breath as bitcoin settles into a holding pattern amid July rally
item.description: BTC is rangebound between $64,000 and $66,800 after a 13% recovery from July's lows, with macro markets offering little direction and WLFI the session's surprise standout at +12%....

item.title: New msaRAT malware uses Chrome, Edge browsers to route C2 traffic
item.description: The Chaos ransomware gang is using a new backdoor dubbed msaRAT that hides command-and-control (C2) communication by routing it through the Chrome or Edge browsers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

Google on Thursday announced a new way for users to sign-in to their accounts by letting them take a selfie video.
The selfie for sign-in, per the tech giant, is another option on top of existing rec...

item.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs
item.description:
RefluXFS, a new Linux kernel flaw disclosed on July 22 and tracked as CVE-2026-64600, lets an unprivileged local user overwrite root-owned files on an XFS filesystem and gain persistent root access.
...

item.title: Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
item.description: A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking what...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
item.description: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less...
item.dc:creator: Krebs On Security

item.title: Milford, New Hampshire Confirms Unauthorized Activity, Withholds Details of Suspected Cyberattack (1)
item.description: Milford, New Hampshire is a quintessential New England town. But charm is no defense against cyberattackers, and it appears that the town may have been attacked last week. As DysruptionHub was the fir...

item.title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices.
item.description: Hannah Murphy reports: Nearly half of companies that are targets of a ransomware cyber attack end up paying a ransom to release their data or systems, according to 2025 research from cybersecurity gro...

item.title: Ransomware Is Accelerating, but It's Not Because of AI

item.title: Using LLMs to Find and Prioritize Vulnerabilities Is No Easy Task

item.title: Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug That Exposed Real Addresses in Mail Logs
item.description:
Apple has moved to address a security flaw in its Hide My Email service that enabled users' real email addresses to be unmasked, effectively undermining the feature's privacy guarantees.
404 Media re...

item.title: Cardano’s NIGHT Hits All-Time Low After 290M Token Dump
